content: Warren's SQLite timestamps are stored as ISO8601 TEXT (e.g., agents.registered_at, runs.started_at, events.ts), not drizzle's integer mode. Decided per SPEC §9 sketch — events.ts is 'ISO8601 from burrow envelope', so we don't translate at the stream boundary. Burrow uses integer mode timestamps; warren deliberately diverges. When adding new tables, follow this pattern: text("col_at").notNull() with toISOString() at the call site.

runs.id (warren's run_ id, generated by core/ids.ts) and runs.burrow_run_id (burrow's run_ id, returned by POST /burrows/:id/runs) share the same prefix shape but are independent identities. SPEC §9 calls this out: 'id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, -- run_xxx (warren's, not burrow's)'. When wiring callers, never confuse them: warren run id is what the UI/HTTP API speaks; burrow run id is what we send to burrow's stream/cancel/inbox endpoints.

title: Inline state-machine in repos/runs.ts
rationale: Warren currently has only one state machine (runs: queued/running/succeeded/failed/cancelled), so ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS + assertRunTransition live in src/db/repos/runs.ts rather than a separate core/state-machine.ts (the pattern burrow uses with two state machines). Extract to core/state-machine.ts when a second state machine appears (e.g., projects clone/active/archived, schedules enabled/disabled in V2).

name: drizzle migration filename pinning
description: drizzle-kit generate emits 0000_<random>.sql by default. We rename to 0000_init.sql and update src/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json's 'tag' field to match ("tag": "0000_init"). The migrate runner reads the filename via the journal, so both must agree. Repeat the rename + journal edit for every future migration; pick a stable, descriptive tag (0001_add_schedules, etc.). Burrow uses the same convention.

Migration 0003 (orphan_runs_on_project_delete) used the canonical SQLite '12-step ALTER' pattern with PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF/ON inside the migration body. drizzle-orm wraps each migration in BEGIN/COMMIT, and SQLite silently ignores PRAGMA foreign_keys toggled inside a multi-statement transaction. With FK still ON, DROP TABLE runs failed against any production DB that had events rows referencing runs (SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY at migrator.js).
resolution: Hoist the pragma out of the migration: src/db/client.ts sets PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF on the raw connection BEFORE migrate() opens its transaction, then PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON after migrate() returns. The migration's own PRAGMA lines stay (drizzle-kit auto-generates them; harmless no-ops). Added regression test in src/db/client.test.ts that builds a partial migrations folder (0000-0002), populates events with FK-referencing rows, then re-opens with the full folder — the test reproduces the SQLite error without the fix.

content: PRAGMA foreign_keys cannot be toggled inside a transaction (SQLite silently ignores it). Drizzle's migrate() runs each migration body inside BEGIN/COMMIT, so any FK-disabling pragma needed for the SQLite '12-step ALTER' rebuild pattern (CREATE __new / INSERT SELECT / DROP / RENAME) MUST be set on the raw connection before migrate() runs, not inside the .sql file. src/db/client.ts owns that toggle: FK=OFF before migrate, FK=ON after.

RUN_FAILURE_REASONS (and similar drizzle enum unions) is a TS-only narrowing — text({ enum }) does not generate a SQL CHECK constraint, so adding a new enum value is a one-line schema.ts edit with no migration needed. The SQL column stays plain text.

runs.pr_url (warren-f6af, migration 0004_add_run_pr_url) is a nullable text column reap fills in via repos.runs.setPrUrl(...) before the terminal transition. Persisted independently from finalize() so the URL also lands on the reap.completed event payload.

triggers table PK is a composite string '<projectId>:<triggerId>' (single text column, not a multi-column PK) — the trigger's authoring identity in .warren/triggers.yaml is the durable handle, so the scheduler tick writes back state without juggling a generated id. Use makeTriggerRowId(projectId, triggerId) from src/db/schema.ts to build the key consistently.

TriggersRepo.upsert uses undefined-vs-null semantics on patch fields: omitted (undefined) preserves the existing column, explicit null clears it. Same shape as RunsRepo.attachBurrow. The scheduler can write a fresh nextFireAt across many ticks without disturbing the lastFiredAt/lastRunId stamped at fire time.

warren workers table (src/db/schema.ts, warren-b0a3) holds {name PK, url, state, addedAt} with state enum WORKER_STATES = ['healthy','draining','unreachable'] (default 'healthy'); bearer token is NOT a column — single shared BURROW_API_TOKEN env across the pool (plan pl-9ba1 alternative #3). Zero-row table is the steady state for single-worker deploys; BurrowClientPool synthesizes a local row from WARREN_BURROW_* env vars when empty.

WorkersRepo.upsert (src/db/repos/workers.ts, warren-b0a3) preserves an existing row's state when 'state' is omitted from the patch — config reloads (step 7 [workers] block) must NOT clobber probe/drain-derived liveness. Pass state explicitly to override. Also preserves addedAt across re-registration; url updates in place.

warren burrows table (src/db/schema.ts, warren-135b) is the warren-side {burrow_id → worker_id} placement registry. (id PK, worker_id text NOT NULL, added_at) with index on worker_id. worker_id is plain text — no FK to workers.name — because the zero-config single-worker deploy uses a synthetic local worker not materialized in the workers table. BurrowsRepo.create has no upsert; collisions throw (one burrow id per provisionBurrow call by construction). Source of truth for placeForBurrow sticky-by-burrow lookups.

runs.worker_id (src/db/schema.ts, warren-135b) is the denormalized copy of burrows.worker_id written at run-create time so streaming / cancel / steer paths route to the owning worker without joining burrows. Nullable for back-compat with rows written before pl-9ba1 step 4 wired this in. Composite index runs_worker_state_idx covers least-loaded queries (RunsRepo.countInflightByWorker). attachBurrow accepts workerId in its partial-patch shape alongside burrowId / burrowRunId.

title: Repo layer is async-first (Promise-returning methods)
rationale: Step 1 of R-13 (pl-f17e) converts every public method on the 7 repos to Promise<T>. SQLite backend keeps bun-sqlite's sync .run/.get/.all internally; the outer 'async' wrapper auto-resolves, so behavior is unchanged for callers that await. WarrenDb.close() also becomes async to keep the upcoming pg.Pool.end() shutdown contract uniform across dialects. ~51 production call sites + ~30 test files pick up await keywords; consumers' helper functions (placeForProject/placeForBurrow, bootBridges, buildTriggerSummaries, mergeMulchFile, recoverActiveRunStreams, seedBuiltinAgents, BurrowClientPool.fromEnv/fromConfig/placeFor/clientFor) cascade to async too. Drizzle's transaction callback stays synchronous on the SQLite path; the warren-side seam (the public method surface) is the async contract.

name: Repo method async wrapping for sqlite path
description: Every repo method body keeps the synchronous drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite calls (.run/.get/.all/.transaction(sync-cb)) verbatim — only the function signature is 'async'. Returning the sync value from an 'async function' auto-wraps it in Promise.resolve, so the wire behavior is unchanged on the sqlite path. Methods that previously called this.<method>(...) internally need 'await' (e.g. require() awaits get()) because the inner method now also returns a Promise. The transaction callback itself stays sync — drizzle bun-sqlite's transaction signature is (tx => T), not (tx => Promise<T>); the pg path in step 3 will accept (tx => Promise<T>) per drizzle-orm/node-postgres.

Mechanical edit of mergeMulchFile/mirrorClosedSeeds during async refactor accidentally dropped the 'changed = true' assignment on the 'closed-update' (LWW-newer) branch in src/runs/reap.ts mirrorClosedSeeds. Test 'mirrors closed seeds into the project's .seeds/issues.jsonl via HttpClient.files.read' caught it: the mirror counted closed += 1 but never set changed = true, so the writeFile guard skipped persisting the merged jsonl.
resolution: Restored the 'changed = true' line on the update branch. Trinity (test/lint/typecheck) all green. Lesson: when adding 'await' before existing sequential statements, scrutinize each line in the block — Edit's old_string/new_string diff is easy to misalign when there's nearby similar code (the 'added' branch above also had changed=true).

title: Schema dialect split: shared columns module + parallel sqlite/postgres tables
rationale: R-13 (pl-f17e step 2). Drizzle has no shared sqlite/pg table builder — pgTable and sqliteTable live in different modules with different column functions. Layout: src/db/schema/columns.ts (dialect-agnostic enum tuples + table/index name constants + makeTriggerRowId), src/db/schema/sqlite.ts (sqliteTables; today's runtime), src/db/schema/postgres.ts (pgTable mirror; lit up by step 3 warren-a66e), src/db/schema.ts (re-export shim that does 'export * from columns.ts' + 'export * from sqlite.ts' so every existing repo/consumer import keeps working). Postgres tables are build-only until openDatabase becomes dialect-aware; the drift test catches divergence.

content: Postgres column-type mapping rules for the sqlite ↔ pg mirror (src/db/schema/postgres.ts). text({mode:'json'}) → jsonb (binary storage, no string round-trip; same TS infer of unknown). real → doublePrecision (sqlite REAL is 8-byte IEEE; pg 'real' is 4-byte single precision and would lose accuracy on costUsd). integer().primaryKey({autoIncrement:true}) → serial().primaryKey() (pg SERIAL = int + sequence). Text-enum columns stay text({enum}) in both — mx-2ab984 TS-only narrowing, no SQL CHECK. Column names, FK targets/onDelete, and index names are byte-identical via columns.ts constants and the drift test.

name: Schema drift parity test (sqlite ↔ postgres)
description: src/db/schema/drift.test.ts uses drizzle's getTableConfig from both pg-core and sqlite-core to assert column lists / nullability / primary keys / FK targets+onDelete / index names+columns match across the two dialect modules. Two normalization gotchas: (1) pg-core wraps index columns in an IndexedColumn proxy that is NOT a PgColumn instance, so 'is(col, PgColumn)' fails — duck-type via .name instead, and use a '<sql>' marker for sql`...` template fragments. (2) sqlite-core leaves fk.onUpdate undefined when unspecified; pg-core defaults it to 'no action' — coalesce undefined → 'no action' on both sides before comparing. Type-level differences (jsonb vs text mode:json, doublePrecision vs real, serial vs integer-autoincrement) are sanctioned and intentionally not compared.

title: WARREN_DB_URL env contract: dialect-aware database URL
rationale: R-13 (pl-f17e step 3, warren-a66e). Single env-var (WARREN_DB_URL) selects the storage backend: 'sqlite:///path' / 'file:///path' / ':memory:' / bare path → bun-sqlite Database; 'postgres://...' / 'postgresql://...' → pg.Pool. The bare-path back-compat surface keeps WARREN_DB_PATH operators (and today's tests passing 'path' to openDatabase) unbroken — both flow through parseDatabaseUrl (src/db/url.ts) and the same dispatch in openDatabase. WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX (default 10) tunes pg.Pool({ max }); sqlite ignores. Step 5 (warren-e2ea) wires the env into server/CLI/doctor.

name: openDatabase overload narrowing: { path } stays SQLite-typed, { url } returns AnyWarrenDb
description: src/db/client.ts uses TS overloads to keep the pre-R-13 sqlite signature back-compat: openDatabase({ path }) returns SqliteWarrenDb (== type alias WarrenDb) — no narrowing needed by today's repos/tests that all call new ProjectsRepo(db.drizzle) etc. The new openDatabase({ url }) overload returns AnyWarrenDb = SqliteWarrenDb | PostgresWarrenDb; callers narrow on db.dialect. Production boot (server/main.ts) still passes { path } so the SQLite branch is the only runtime-exercised path until step 5 widens the boot signature. The Postgres branch is callable but skips migrations gracefully when src/db/migrations/postgres/ doesn't exist (step 4 populates that folder).

content: src/db/url.ts parseDatabaseUrl trims input, special-cases ':memory:', recognizes postgres/postgresql/sqlite/file schemes (case-insensitive), and falls back to bare-path → sqlite for back-compat with WARREN_DB_PATH. sqlite URL slash counting: 3 slashes after the scheme → absolute path ('sqlite:///data/warren.db' → '/data/warren.db'); 2 slashes → relative ('sqlite://./rel' → './rel'); 0 slashes → in-memory sentinel ('sqlite::memory:' → ':memory:'). 1 slash ('sqlite:/foo') is documented as relative; operators should prefer 3-slash absolute or 0-slash sentinel. sqliteUrlForPath(p) is the inverse — round-trips through parseDatabaseUrl.

title: Per-dialect drizzle.config pair, regen pg migrations from scratch as single 0000_init
rationale: R-13 (pl-f17e step 4, warren-373e). Drizzle-kit emits one migration per generate invocation based on diff vs latest snapshot. Two viable approaches for the initial pg migration set: (A) generate a single 0000_init that builds the final schema state; (B) replay each sqlite history step (0000-0008) against successive schema checkouts to mint a parallel pg journal. Chose (A) because pg has no analog to sqlite's 12-step ALTER pattern (mx-9c90e8), nobody operates a pg warren today that needs to walk through the historical states, and the parity gate (acceptance #7) is about FUTURE migrations matching tag-for-tag, not bytewise replay of history. Single 0000_init.sql + meta/0000_snapshot.json + version-7 _journal.json with dialect: 'postgresql'. Layout: drizzle.config.sqlite.ts (sqlite, --config explicit) + drizzle.config.postgres.ts (postgresql, --config explicit); package.json adds db:generate:sqlite, db:generate:postgres, and a db:generate that runs both. Deleted the unscoped drizzle.config.ts that previously pointed at the schema.ts re-export shim so drizzle-kit doesn't see duplicated tables.

content: Postgres migrations live under src/db/migrations/postgres/ with their own meta/_journal.json (version 7, dialect 'postgresql'). The two dialect journals are independent — sqlite version-6 entries don't translate, and tags don't need to match historically. Future schema changes must emit BOTH dialects in lockstep via 'bun run db:generate' (which chains db:generate:sqlite && db:generate:postgres); the CI parity gate (step 9 docs) enforces matching tags going forward. Generated files (.sql + meta/*.json) must be biome-formatted after generate per mx-e078d8 (drizzle-kit emits 2-space, biome enforces tabs).

Per-dialect drizzle config: drizzle.config.sqlite.ts and drizzle.config.postgres.ts each point at the dialect-specific schema module ('./src/db/schema/sqlite.ts' and './src/db/schema/postgres.ts' respectively), NOT at the re-export shim src/db/schema.ts. The shim re-exports both columns.ts (dialect-agnostic constants) and sqlite.ts; pointing drizzle-kit at it confused the generator into emitting duplicate-looking output. Use 'drizzle-kit generate --config=drizzle.config.<dialect>.ts'. The previously unscoped drizzle.config.ts was removed.

title: Boot wiring on WARREN_DB_URL across server, CLI, doctor
rationale: R-13 (pl-f17e step 5, warren-e2ea). loadServerConfigFromEnv now exposes dbUrl (string) plus dbUrlConflict (string|null) instead of dbPath; precedence is WARREN_DB_URL > sqliteUrlForPath(WARREN_DB_PATH) > sqliteUrlForPath(<DATA_DIR>/warren.db). CLI's withCliDb / resolveDbUrl apply the same precedence. bootServer + withCliDb open via openDatabase({ url }) and the AnyWarrenDb is narrowed to sqlite at the createRepos boundary — the repo layer is sqlite-only until step 7 (warren-480a) widens it, so a pg URL fail-fasts at boot with a pointer to the unfinished step. WARREN_DB_PATH stays as a back-compat alias and conflicts surface as a logger.warn (server) / doctor warren_db ok=false (CLI). The boot log line now reports {dbUrl: <redacted>, dialect}.

name: pingDatabase: dialect-aware SELECT-1 probe
description: src/db/client.ts exports pingDatabase(db: AnyWarrenDb): Promise<void>. sqlite branch is synchronous (db.raw.query('SELECT 1 AS one').get()) wrapped in an async function so callers always await; postgres branch awaits db.raw.query('SELECT 1') against the pg pool. Throws on connection failure — callers (checkDatabaseReachable in diagnostics/checks.ts) catch and shape into the {ok, message, hint} envelope. Used by /readyz db_reachable and warren doctor db_reachable to satisfy R-13 pl-f17e acceptance #2.

title: Test substrate: env-gated WARREN_TEST_PG_URL (pglite/testcontainers deferred)
rationale: R-13 (pl-f17e step 6, warren-c823) picks the simplest substrate that exercises warren's real pg path: an env-gated WARREN_TEST_PG_URL pointed at a real Postgres. pglite (in-process WASM pg) considered but rejected for V1 — adds a new runtime dep, WASM/Bun compat unproven here, and it's single-connection so it can't exercise the pg.Pool starvation scenario step 7 needs. testcontainers reserved for the acceptance harness (warren-480a) where end-to-end realism matters. Local dev defaults to sqlite (no friction); CI's pg matrix sets both env vars in a job with a services:postgres block. Zero new deps, real pg semantics, opt-in coverage.

name: withDb(): dialect-aware test handle with pg schema isolation
description: src/db/testing.ts exports withDb() returning a TestDbHandle with Symbol.asyncDispose. SQLite path: openDatabase({ path: ':memory:' }) — the { path } overload narrows to SqliteWarrenDb without a runtime branch. Postgres path: each call mints a unique 'warren_test_<8hex>' schema, opens openDatabase({ url, skipMigrations: true, pgPoolMax: 2 }) with options=-c search_path=<schema> appended via appendPgConnectionOption(), then runs migratePg with migrationsSchema pinned to the test schema so __drizzle_migrations also lives inside it. close() DROP SCHEMA <name> CASCADE reaps everything. Tests use 'await using handle = await withDb()' or 'try/finally await handle.close()'. resolveTestDialect() reads WARREN_TEST_DIALECT (case-insensitive: sqlite/postgres/postgresql); isPostgresTestEnabled() gates pg-only describe blocks via describe.skipIf(!isPostgresTestEnabled()).

content: Test schema isolation for pg uses search_path startup option, not per-query qualification. Pool URL gets ?options=-c%20search_path%3D<schema> appended; every connection in the pool applies it at startup so drizzle queries land in <schema> without rewriting SQL. migratePg() takes migrationsSchema: <schema> so its __drizzle_migrations bookkeeping table is also inside the test schema and dies with DROP SCHEMA CASCADE.

name: Cross-dialect row passthrough: sqlite select → pg insert without field mapping
description: Because schema/sqlite.ts and schema/postgres.ts are kept byte-identical at the column-list/index level by the drift test (mx-de2250), and the column-type mapping (mx-655320) preserves JS-side shapes (text mode:'json' ↔ jsonb both materialize as deserialized JS values; real ↔ doublePrecision both are JS number; integer ↔ integer/serial both are number; text enums stay text in both), a row selected via source.drizzle.select().from(sqliteSchema.X).all() can be passed directly to target.drizzle.insert(pgSchema.X).values(rows) with no per-column conversion. The migrate-to-postgres tool (src/cli/commands/db.ts) relies on this; future dialect-spanning tooling can do the same. Note: the source dialect is sqlite and target is pg in this direction; the reverse (pg → sqlite) would need TEXT round-trips for jsonb (and may not be lossless on cost_usd subnormals).

content: events.id serial sequence must be advanced after a bulk INSERT that supplies explicit ids — SELECT setval(pg_get_serial_sequence('events','id'), (SELECT MAX(id) FROM events)) — or the next native append (drizzle's omit-id insert) will collide with the seeded PKs. The migrate-to-postgres tool (src/cli/commands/db.ts) does this once at the end of the events copy; skipped when no events were inserted because setval(seq, NULL) errors. Same pattern applies to any future bulk loader that round-trips serial PKs.

description: withDb({dialect:'postgres'}) cannot run warren's pg migrations in an isolated per-test schema because src/db/migrations/postgres/0000_init.sql embeds literal "public"."<parent>" qualifiers on every FK constraint (e.g. 'REFERENCES "public"."runs"("id")'). Even with search_path=warren_test_xxx set via connection options + migrationsSchema:schemaName on the drizzle migrator, the literal 'public' references resolve outside the test schema and fail with 'relation public.runs does not exist'. drizzle-kit doesn't have an option to suppress the schema qualifier on FK targets — the SQL is captured at generate time, not migrate time.
resolution: Workaround at the test layer: provision a fresh DATABASE per test via a maintenance Client (CREATE DATABASE / DROP DATABASE WITH FORCE), same shape as acceptance scenario 19. Migrations land cleanly in the new database's default public schema, and isolation comes from the database boundary instead of a schema boundary. src/cli/commands/db.test.ts uses this pattern. The withDb pg path stays broken until either drizzle emits unqualified FK SQL or the migrations are regenerated with a schema-aware drizzle-kit option.

title: Preview timestamp columns stay TEXT in both dialects, not TIMESTAMPTZ
rationale: SPEC §11.L line 1240-1241 nominally maps preview_started_at and preview_last_hit_at to TIMESTAMPTZ on postgres, but the existing convention (started_at, ended_at, last_fetched_at, added_at, etc.) uses TEXT in both dialects. Diverging would break the cross-dialect row-passthrough invariant (mx-845f11) that src/cli/commands/db.ts depends on — drizzle-orm returns a Date object for timestamptz but a string for text, so a sqlite select → pg insert would no longer be field-mapping-free. Decision: store ISO8601 strings in TEXT for both dialects, matching every other timestamp column in the schema. Schema drift test (mx-de2250) stays green naturally.

content: Drizzle bun-sqlite update().run() returns void in TypeScript types — the underlying bun:sqlite Changes shape (.changes:number) is not exposed. To check rowsAffected from app code, do a select-then-update inside drizzle.transaction({behavior:'immediate'}) so the read serializes with concurrent writers (mx-3f18fc allocator pattern). Preview eviction worker (warren-ea6b) uses this CAS to stay re-entrant with manual teardown.

RunPreviewsRepo (src/preview/eviction.ts) has TWO BEGIN IMMEDIATE CAS methods that share serialization with the port allocator (mx-3f18fc): evict() flips starting/live → torn-down and returns boolean (used by the LRU eviction worker); claimTeardown() does the same flip but returns {status:'torn-down'|'already-torn-down'|'already-failed'|'never-launched', previousState, port, burrowId} so the manual teardown route (warren-d725) can shape JSON + decide whether to emit preview_torn_down. Either method racing the other lands in exactly one — the loser sees 'already-torn-down' from claimTeardown or false from evict. A missing row routes through 'never-launched' rather than throwing inside the txn.

title: Preview port allocator uses runs table as the only source of truth (no separate state table)
rationale: SPEC §11.L picked SQLite-backed restart-safe allocation derived directly from runs.preview_port + preview_state IN ('starting','live'). Alternatives (a separate preview_ports table, an in-memory map persisted to disk, a free-port range tracker) all duplicate state that the runs table already carries. With the chosen design, a fresh PreviewPortAllocator on the same db sees in-use ports via SELECT preview_port FROM runs WHERE preview_state IN ('starting','live') — restart-safe with zero migration cost. Release means another path (eviction worker, manual teardown) sets preview_state to 'torn-down' or 'failed'; the next SELECT naturally omits the port via the state filter, so even a stale preview_port left behind on a 'failed' row is correctly treated as free. BEGIN IMMEDIATE serializes concurrent allocators against the same db. Default range 30000-31000 via WARREN_PREVIEW_PORT_RANGE; exhaustion emits preview_failed with reason='port_exhausted' and a doctor warning fires at ≥80% saturation.

title: Dialect-polymorphic drizzle adapter centralizes 56 sqlite-sync call sites
rationale: R-13 (pl-f1be step 1) introduces src/db/repos/drizzle-adapter.ts as a thin executor exposing pickOne/pickAll/runWrite/runReturningOne/runReturningAll/runInTransaction. Chosen over (a) duplicating each repo as Sqlite*Repo/Postgres*Repo, which doubles code+tests and drifts schema, and (b) inlining the dialect-agnostic pattern across all 56 call sites, which fans dispatch and is regression-prone. The adapter is ~150 LOC, auditable in isolation, and unit-tested against both backends via withDb (sqlite always; pg under WARREN_TEST_PG_URL). Repos in pl-f1be steps 2-5 migrate one at a time atop this surface.

name: sqlite async transaction via raw BEGIN/COMMIT exec
description: drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite's db.transaction(fn) is synchronous — if fn returns a Promise, drizzle commits before it resolves. To accept the dialect-agnostic (tx) => Promise<T> signature, DrizzleAdapter.runInTransaction issues BEGIN / COMMIT / ROLLBACK directly via raw.exec on the bun:sqlite connection. Safe because warren is single-event-loop and single-connection on sqlite — no SQL can interleave between BEGIN and COMMIT/ROLLBACK on the same connection. The pg branch delegates to drizzle's native async db.transaction(fn) and rebuilds an inner adapter around the tx-scoped handle.

content: pg drizzle INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE without .returning() resolves to a pg.QueryResult, NOT a row array. Only .select() and .returning() builders resolve to T[]. The dialect-polymorphic adapter therefore splits its input types: Readable<T> = SqliteReadable<T> | PromiseLike<T[]> for pickOne/pickAll/runReturning*; Writable = SqliteWritable | PromiseLike<unknown> for runWrite. Conflating them (e.g. PgRunnable<T> = PromiseLike<T[]>) makes runWrite calls fail typecheck against pg insert builders.

DrizzleAdapter exposes drizzle as the AnyDrizzleHandle union (SqliteDrizzleDb | PostgresDrizzleDb) but TypeScript can't call .select()/.insert()/.update()/.delete() on the union because drizzle's per-dialect signatures (SQLiteSelectBase vs PgSelectBase, sqliteTable vs pgTable) are mutually incompatible. The repos in pl-f1be steps 2-5 will need to narrow dialect at the boundary before building queries, OR the adapter will need a follow-up to expose dialect-correct query-building shortcuts. For step 1, tests narrow per dialect: separate describe blocks for sqlite (using sqliteSchema tables) and pg (using pgSchema tables) — duplication is meaningful because pg jsonb rejects sqlite's text-mode JSON serialization (mx-655320).

DrizzleAdapter.schema returns the dialect-correct schema module (sqliteSchema | pgSchema) typed as WarrenSchema (= typeof sqliteSchema). The cast is the only place in the codebase where the dialect-incorrect type is tolerated — both modules export structurally identical identifiers (agents, projects, runs, events, triggers, workers, burrows) so consumers can build queries against the sqlite-shaped type while the runtime object dispatches against the correct dialect. Drizzle's db.insert(table) / db.select().from(table) check entityKind at runtime (SQLiteTable vs PgTable have different classes), so the table MUST match the handle's dialect — that's why the adapter binds them together at construction.

name: dialect-polymorphic-repo
description: Repos on the DrizzleAdapter pattern (AgentsRepo, BurrowsRepo as of pl-f1be step 2): constructor takes a DrizzleAdapter; private 'db' getter casts adapter.drizzle as SqliteDrizzleDb (TS-only; runtime is the dialect-correct handle); private getters bind to adapter.schema.* tables (runtime-correct, typed as sqlite shape); reads use adapter.pickOne / pickAll, writes use adapter.runWrite, transactions use adapter.runInTransaction with the same casting pattern via tx.drizzle / tx.schema. All call surfaces stay sqlite-shaped at the type level; the adapter's dispatch makes them run on either dialect.

content: Postgres count(*) is bigint, returned as string by node-postgres — repo code MUST coerce with Number() before returning a number to callers. Drizzle does not auto-cast bigint columns. EventsRepo.countByRun (and earlier RunsRepo.countInflightByWorker) take this shape: pickOne<{ n: number | string }>(...) then return Number(row?.n ?? 0). Same applies to any sum()/count() aggregate; max() over an integer column already returns number, no coercion needed.

When a dialect-polymorphic repo test needs a foreign-key row whose owning repo has NOT yet migrated to the adapter (mid-pl-f1be), seed the FK target directly through the adapter (adapter.runWrite(db.insert(adapter.schema.runs).values({...}))) instead of constructing the pre-migrated repo. EventsRepo tests do this for the runs FK so they can parameterize over sqlite + postgres ahead of RunsRepo's own step-5 migration.

description: TriggersRepo + WorkersRepo were the first migration to the dialect-polymorphic adapter that involved a transaction-wrapping repo with a caller (src/triggers/dispatch.test.ts) that had been passing the old sync-transaction repo without await. Under the original sync db.transaction((tx)=>{...}) the entire transaction body completed before the unawaited promise was returned, so the missing await was invisible. The async runInTransaction yields after raw.exec('BEGIN'), so subsequent code running on the next tick's path can call upsert again and hit 'cannot start a transaction within a transaction' on the still-open BEGIN.
resolution: Add the missing await to the seed-setup call in dispatch.test.ts:178. When migrating any repo with a transaction wrapper, grep all callers for unawaited promises (rg --multiline 'repos\.\w+\.upsert\b' | grep -v await) — the test runner won't catch them on the old sync path.

content: Tests for repos that depend on a not-yet-migrated FK target (e.g. TriggersRepo needs runs.id; RunsRepo migrates in pl-f1be step 5) MUST seed the FK row directly through the adapter, not via the not-yet-migrated repo. Pattern: const runId = generateId('run'); const db = adapter.drizzle as SqliteDrizzleDb; await adapter.runWrite(db.insert(adapter.schema.runs).values({...minimal fields...})); — same workaround events.test.ts uses. Calling the not-yet-migrated repo would crash on postgres because its body still uses sqlite-only sync .run()/.get() APIs.

RunsRepo migrated to DrizzleAdapter (pl-f1be step 5, warren-1d3a): claimById's previous synchronous db.transaction() now goes through adapter.runInTransaction (sqlite branch raw BEGIN/COMMIT, see mx-d5cf19). createRepos in src/db/repos/index.ts wires all 7 repos onto the adapter — the per-repo dialect-polymorphic-repo pattern (mx-1d9f7a) is fully applied. Next: step 7 (warren-d2fd) widens createRepos's input from WarrenDb to AnyWarrenDb and removes the createReposForDialect/withCliDb guards.

Sibling repo tests that previously inline-seeded a runs row to satisfy an FK (events.test.ts, triggers.test.ts) now use RunsRepo.create directly — since pl-f1be step 5 landed, RunsRepo is on the adapter and works in both dialects. The workaround pattern in mx-edec77 (inline-insert through adapter.runWrite to dodge a not-yet-migrated FK target) is no longer needed within this codebase; keep the technique in mind for future incremental migrations.

title: createRepos widened to AnyWarrenDb; dialect guards removed
rationale: pl-f1be step 7 (warren-d2fd): all 7 repos now run through DrizzleAdapter (steps 1-5) so createRepos can accept AnyWarrenDb without the prior sqlite-only narrowing. The two fail-fast guards introduced for the half-shipped R-13 dogfood (server/main.ts createReposForDialect and cli/context.ts withCliDb's dialect !== sqlite check) are deleted. Preview port allocator and eviction worker stay gated on dialect === sqlite per plan risk #4 — they use raw sqlite BEGIN IMMEDIATE and need their own pg branch (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE) before they can widen.

content: Postgres dialect: warren tables in 'public' must have RLS enabled (migration 0002_enable_rls.sql) so PostgREST anon/authenticated traffic on Supabase deployments is deny-all. Warren itself connects as the postgres superuser via WARREN_DB_URL and bypasses RLS for owners/superusers (no FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY), so app code is unaffected. Hand-rolled migration: ALTER TABLE ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY for the 7 tables (agents, burrows, events, projects, runs, triggers, workers), plus a journal entry (idx:2, tag:0002_enable_rls) and a 0002_snapshot.json copied from 0001 with a fresh UUID and prevId pointing at 0001's id. RLS is not modeled in src/db/schema/postgres.ts (no .enableRLS() call) — drift is intentional and tolerated since the drift test only compares column shape. SQLite path is untouched (RLS is pg-only).

runs.seed_id (sqlite migration 0010_add_run_seed_id, postgres migration 0003_add_run_seed_id, pl-bb70 step 3 / warren-805a) is a nullable text column with no FK — seeds live in the project workspace JSONL, not in warren's database, and the seed-id space is per-project. Populated at run-create time when POST /runs supplies seedId; null for legacy rows and manual prompts. Drift test (src/db/schema/drift.test.ts) keeps the sqlite/pg shapes in lockstep.

description: drizzle-kit v0.20+ (or however it generates ORDER-BY-style index columns) emits a CREATE INDEX on a rebuilt table where the descending column is backquoted as a whole: `runs_project_started_idx ON runs (`project_id`,`"started_at" DESC`)` — the backquotes treat "started_at" DESC as a single column identifier, which SQLite can't resolve. Older 0000_init / 0003 migrations emit the correct `(`project_id`,"started_at" DESC)` shape.
resolution: Hand-edit the generated migration to remove the outer backticks around the DESC expression. The snapshot stores the broken identifier verbatim ("\"started_at\" DESC"), but that's only data drizzle reads back to compute the next diff — the migration SQL is what executes against the DB. Future regenerations of any runs-table rebuild will need the same hand-edit.

name: agents three-tier identity via composite + partial unique indexes
description: agents table no longer keys by name. R-03 (pl-fef5, warren-094a) replaces the name PK with a synthetic rowid (integer autoIncrement in sqlite, serial in pg). Identity is enforced by two unique indexes: composite `(project_id, name)` for project-tier rows, plus a partial unique `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL` to enforce one global row per name (sqlite/pg treat NULL as distinct in plain unique indexes, so the composite alone is insufficient). `runs.agent_name` FK to `agents.name` was dropped — composite identity is unrepresentable as a single-column FK; the agents table is a soft cache (POST /agents/refresh re-discovers from canopy).

content: AgentsRepo (src/db/repos/agents.ts, R-03 pl-fef5 step 1) scopes every read/write to the global tier via `and(eq(agents.name, name), isNull(agents.projectId))`. The repo manages only project_id IS NULL rows; project-tier rows will land via step 5's refreshProjectAgents(), and scope-aware accessors arrive in step 3 (warren-2f14).

AgentsRepo (src/db/repos/agents.ts) is scope-aware as of R-03 step 3 (pl-fef5, warren-2f14). Every read/write takes an optional AgentScope ({projectId?: string | null}). Omitted or null projectId targets the global tier (project_id IS NULL); a string targets that project exactly. Surface: upsert({name, projectId?, renderedJson, now?}); get(name, scope?) and require(name, scope?) are exact-match (no fallback); resolve(name, scope?) is project-first with global fallback when projectId is set (used by spawn in step 7 / warren-0a7e); listAll(scope?) returns global-only by default and global∪project when projectId is set; listForProject(projectId) returns just that project's tier (used by refreshProjectAgents in step 5); delete(name, scope?) is scoped. The default-global behavior preserves all 50+ pre-R-03 call sites (handlers.ts, spawn.ts, every test that does repos.agents.upsert({name, renderedJson})) without edits.

name: scope-where helper for agents (name, projectId)
description: Composite identity on agents.(name, project_id) means every scoped query needs a where clause that splits on null vs string projectId — drizzle has no eq(col, null), so isNull(col) and eq(col, val) are not interchangeable. AgentsRepo (src/db/repos/agents.ts) factors this into a single scopeWhere(agents, name, projectId: string|null) helper: returns and(eq(name), isNull(projectId)) when projectId is null and and(eq(name), eq(projectId, val)) otherwise. Used by upsert (both lookup and update where-clauses), get, require, and delete. The helper takes the agents table reference so it can be reused from within runInTransaction (which exposes tx.schema.agents) without capturing the repo's outer-scope this.agents.

content: AgentsRepo.listAll(scope?) returns rows ordered by name ASC without deduplicating across tiers — when scope.projectId is set, both the global (project_id=NULL) and project-tier rows for a same-named agent appear in the result. Dedupe-with-project-tier-precedence belongs at the HTTP/UI layer (pl-fef5 step 6 / warren-7777 owns it), not in the repo. AgentsRepo.resolve(name, scope?) is the project-first-with-global-fallback shape spawn (step 7) needs and returns a single row; do NOT use listAll for resolution semantics.

projects.has_plot (sqlite migration 0012_grey_otto_octavius, postgres migration 0005_nappy_wraith, warren-4e20) is a NOT NULL boolean defaulted false. sqlite stores it as integer with drizzle mode:'boolean'; postgres uses native boolean. Drift parity test compares structure (name/nullability/default-presence) not storage type, so the int-vs-bool difference is sanctioned. Populated by addProject (initial probe after clone) and recordRefresh (re-probed every git fetch); refreshProject threads result.features.hasPlot into the repo call. Legacy rows from before migration 0012 get false on backfill.

runs.plot_id (sqlite migration 0013_secret_malcolm_colcord, postgres migration 0006_loud_vapor, warren-a8c3) is the per-run back-link to the Plot the dispatch was bound to. Nullable text, no FK (Plots live in the project workspace, not warren's db). Index runs_plot_id_idx covers Plot-affinity lookups. Set once at spawn via RunsRepo.CreateRunInput.plotId; never mutates.

projects.has_seeds (sqlite migration 0014_add_project_has_seeds, postgres migration 0007_add_project_has_seeds, warren-9990 / pl-a258 step 1) mirrors projects.has_plot (mx-96f004): BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false in both dialects, populated by detectProjectFeatures' .seeds/ probe in addProject + refreshProjectClone (src/projects/refresh.ts). ProjectsRepo.create + recordRefresh thread hasSeeds the same way as hasPlot. The PlanRun API (warren-f923) reads project.hasSeeds to gate plan-run dispatch.

plan_runs (sqlite migration 0015_add_plan_runs, postgres migration 0008_add_plan_runs, warren-4d7c / pl-a258 step 2) holds one row per dispatched sd plan: id TEXT PK (plnr_ prefix), plan_id (seeds pl-XXXX, plain text no FK), project_id FK→projects ON DELETE CASCADE, agent_name, prompt_template DEFAULT 'work on sd {seed_id}' rendered per child via {seed_id} substitution, ref nullable (null→spawn falls back to project.defaultBranch), provider/model_override nullable, dispatcher_handle DEFAULT 'operator', trigger DEFAULT 'manual', state TS-only narrowed to PLAN_RUN_STATES (queued|running|succeeded|failed|cancelled), failure_reason free-form text, created_at/started_at/ended_at ISO8601. Indexes plan_runs_project_state and plan_runs_state; the latter powers the coordinator's listActive() (queued|running) tick walk.

plan_run_children (sqlite migration 0015_add_plan_runs, postgres migration 0008_add_plan_runs, warren-4d7c / pl-a258 step 2) has composite PK (plan_run_id, seq) — drizzle primaryKey({columns:[t.planRunId,t.seq]}) — so the coordinator's pickNextPending(planRunId) gets an O(1) ordered scan via the (plan_run_id,state) index. plan_run_id FK→plan_runs ON DELETE CASCADE; run_id FK→runs ON DELETE SET NULL so deleting a warren run orphans the child without breaking the parent. state TS-only narrowed to PLAN_RUN_CHILD_STATES (pending|dispatched|running|pr_open|merged|failed|skipped) — 'skipped' encodes resume semantics (warren-fcc9: re-dispatching the same plan id advances past already-closed seeds without spawning); 'pr_open' is the post-success pre-merge wait state; 'merged' is the only happy-path terminal. created_at + updatedAt (bumped on every updateChild) + started_at/ended_at/pr_merged_at/failure_reason. Indexes: plan_run_children_run (reverse runId→child for reap path) and plan_run_children_state on (plan_run_id, state).

name: PlanRunsRepo: atomic parent+children insert via DrizzleAdapter tx
description: PlanRunsRepo.create({planRunRow, children}) lands the parent plan_runs row plus N plan_run_children rows in a single DrizzleAdapter.runInTransaction (mx-d5cf19 sqlite-async-tx pattern for sqlite, native drizzle pg tx for postgres). The transaction throws on duplicate seq because the composite PK rejects the second insert (SQLITE_CONSTRAINT / unique-violation in pg), and the rollback ensures the parent never appears without its full child set — verified by plan-runs.test.ts 'create rolls back when a duplicate seq is supplied mid-tx'. State-machine guard lives in transitionTo() mirroring RunsRepo.markRunning/finalize (mx-a5432a inline state-machine), with PLAN_RUN_STATES allowed-transitions table. updateChild() mirrors RunsRepo.attachBurrow's partial-input pattern (mx-0a7a65): omitted fields preserve, explicit null clears, throws ValidationError when no fields supplied; child child state transitions are NOT guarded — the coordinator decides what's legal at any tick, the repo just persists.

content: RunsRepo.listByIds(ids) + EventsRepo.listByRunIds(runIds) (warren-f923) — both use inArray for a single query, return empty array for empty input without a DB roundtrip. listByRunIds orders by (ts asc, id asc) so a UNION snapshot across child runs ships in chronological order for the plan-run events tail.

plan_runs.plot_id (sqlite migration 0016_add_plan_run_plot_id, postgres migration 0009_add_plan_run_plot_id, warren-8bb4 / pl-7937 step 2) is a nullable text column mirroring runs.plot_id (mx-a37ccc) shape exactly: column + plan_runs_plot_id_idx index registered in INDEX_NAMES (src/db/schema/columns.ts). Gated on the owning project's hasPlot at handler edge (Phase 2 step 3, warren-c900). Repo's CreatePlanRunInput accepts optional plotId; create() writes null when absent so existing call-sites stay byte-identical.

RunsRepo.markPaused(runId, questionEventId, now) / markResumedFromPause(runId) (src/db/repos/runs.ts, warren-2976) are the typed transitions for the pause/resume lifecycle. markPaused asserts running→paused via assertRunTransition and stamps paused_at + paused_question_event_id together; markResumedFromPause asserts paused→running and clears BOTH columns so the next pause-detect pass against the same row reads as fresh-running rather than a stale paused remnant. startedAt is preserved across the round-trip — pause is not a terminal state and the burrow run's wall-clock lifetime is unchanged.

RunsRepo.listByPlotId(plotId) (src/db/repos/runs.ts, warren-d22e / pl-0344 step 8) lists every run row bound to a Plot ordered by id asc (ids are random so this is a stable test order, not a time order). Covered by the runs_plot_id index in sqlite + postgres schemas. Caller responsibility: re-sort the underlying events by ts when chronological matters \u2014 the formalize parser does this via extractSuggestedIntent's internal sort. Used by POST /plots/:id/formalize to fan agent_message events across an entire brainstorm conversation in a single query.

RunsRepo.listForAnalytics({projectId?,from?,to?}) (src/db/repos/runs.ts, warren-cf63 / pl-b0c0 step 6) is the cost-analytics endpoint's row fetcher. from/to clip lexicographically on runs.startedAt (ISO8601 is order-preserving); both bounds optional. Ordered by startedAt desc. Use gte/lte from drizzle-orm. PlanRunsRepo.resolvePlanForRunIds(runIds) joins plan_run_children -> plan_runs and returns {runId,planId,planRunId}[] for runs that were dispatched as plan-run children — silently omits non-plan runs so callers treat them as planId=null.

plan_runs.parent_run_id (sqlite migration 0018_add_plan_run_parent_run_id, postgres migration 0011_add_plan_run_parent_run_id, warren-d9a2) is a nullable TEXT column back-linking an auto_plan_run to the run that created it. No FK — the coordinator handles missing parent rows (treat as gate-passed). CreatePlanRunInput gained optional parentRunId field; reap passes run.id when creating auto_plan_runs.

EventsRepo.listToolEventsForRuns(runIds, {limit?}) (src/db/repos/events.ts, warren-e355 / pl-ad0f step 6) returns kind=tool_use/tool_result rows across many runs ordered by (runId, seq) for the run-analytics behavior view (command-mining aggregator correlates tool_result.is_error to tool_use via tool_use_id). Capped at opts.limit (default DEFAULT_TOOL_EVENT_CAP=20000) to bound the events-table scan; empty runIds short-circuits without a DB hit. Mirrors listUsageEvents.

conversations table FKs projects ON DELETE SET NULL (orphan, mirrors runs.project_id); messages FKs conversations ON DELETE CASCADE. MessagesRepo.append auto-allocates monotonic per-conversation seq under a transaction (mirrors EventsRepo per-run seq). Conversation/message ids use generateId('conversation'|'message') -> conv_/msg_ prefixes. Both tables mirrored in sqlite+postgres+drift TABLE_KEYS.

src/db/repos/runs.ts sits at its file-size ceiling (frozen budget 610). New RunsRepo query surface for warren-0b75 (listPrCandidatesByProject, fixAttemptHistoryByPrUrl) lives as standalone functions in src/db/repos/runs-ci-fixer.ts taking the DrizzleAdapter; RunsRepo keeps thin one-line delegating methods so the call surface (repos.runs.X) is unchanged. Dedicated tests live in src/db/repos/runs.ci-fixer.test.ts (own open/spawn harness; tests are jscpd-excluded) to keep runs.test.ts under 500 lines.

plan_run_children.execution_project_id (sqlite migration 0026_cloudy_captain_midlands, postgres migration 0019_silky_alice, warren-57f6 / pl-fb43 step 6) is a nullable text column the coordinator stamps at dispatch with the resolved execution project id (planRun.projectId for an untagged child, or the project the child seed's extensions.repo resolved to). Null on pending/skipped/legacy rows. Plain text, no FK. Added to PlanRunChildPatch + CHILD_PATCH_KEYS in src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts; surfaced on GET /plan-runs/:id child rows and rendered as a Repo column in src/ui/src/pages/plan-run-detail/child-table.tsx; PlanRunDetail relabels the Project meta card to Coordination and adds an Executes across summary.

PlanRunsRepo.listDispatchedPlanIds(projectId) (src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts, warren-34df / pl-3fc4 step 1) returns string[] of distinct plan_id values that already have a plan_run row for the project. selectDistinct({planId}).from(planRuns).where(eq(projectId)); collapses N plan-runs per plan to one, project-scoped so other projects' dispatches never leak. Backs the ready-plans dedup query.

Postgres migration 0020_enable_rls_conversations_messages enables RLS on conversations + messages (mirrors 0002_enable_rls for the original 7 tables). They shipped without RLS in 0015_stiff_mercury. Warren connects via the direct WARREN_DB_URL role which bypasses RLS, so enabling with no anon policies closes the Supabase anon-key read/write hole without breaking warren's own access. RLS is postgres-only — there is intentionally no sqlite sibling migration; migrations.test.ts only enforces journal well-formedness + contiguous idx, not tag-for-tag dialect parity. (warren-062e)

runs.target_branch (sqlite migration 0027_quick_wind_dancer, postgres migration 0021_fine_justin_hammer, warren-1f81 / #419) is a nullable text column persisting the operator-requested target branch at dispatch (Option B), added after pr_url in both schema/sqlite.ts and schema/postgres.ts. CreateRunInput gains targetBranch?: string|null; create() sets targetBranch: input.targetBranch ?? null. After bun run db:generate, the drizzle-generated meta/*.json snapshots + _journal.json must be biome-formatted (bunx biome format --write) or the lint gate fails.
